zh.lifeSummary: 當Robert White於1501年出生在英國哈普爾可利斯丘其時，他的父親Sir Henry White23歲，母親Elizabeth Fenrother21歲。他於1520年與Catherine Barrett在英國英格蘭哈普爾可利斯丘其結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和8個女兒的父母。他去世於1588年在英國哈普爾可利斯丘其享年87歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Robert White于1501年出生在英国哈普爾可利斯丘其时，他的父亲Sir Henry White23岁，母亲Elizabeth Fenrother21岁。他于1520年与Catherine Barrett在英国英格兰哈普爾可利斯丘其结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和8个女儿的父母。他去世于1588年在英国哈普爾可利斯丘其享年87岁。

nameMeanings.name: White
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English white, wit (Old English hwīt ‘white’), hence a nickname for someone with white hair or a pale complexion. In some cases it is perhaps from a Middle English survival of the Old English personal name Hwīta, a short form of names in Hwīt- (from hwīt ‘white’). The name may also be topographic, referring to someone who lived by a bend or curve in a river or road (from Old English wiht ‘bend’), the source of the placename of Great Whyte in Ramsey, Huntingdonshire (compare Wight ). This name is also a variant of Wight . The surname White is also very common among African Americans.

Irish and Scottish: adopted for any of several Irish and Scottish Gaelic names based on bán ‘white, fair’ (see Bain 1, McElwain ) or fionn ‘fair’ (see Finn 1). The English surname has been Gaelicized in Ireland as de Faoite.

Americanized form (translation into English) of various European surnames meaning ‘white’, for example German Weiss , French Blanc , Polish Białas (see Bialas ), Slovenian Belec , or any other synonymous Slavic surname beginning with Bel-, Bev-, Biel- or Bil-.

variants.name: Whitty
meanings: topographic name denoting a ‘dweller by the white hedge or the white (forest) enclosure’, from Middle English whit ‘white’ (Old English hwīt) + heie ‘hedge, enclosure’ (Old English (ge)hæg and Old French haie) perhaps in reference to whitethorn (hawthorn) hedging. This English name has been recorded in Ireland (Wexford) since the 13th century. It has been Gaelicized as de Faoite, see also White .
nickname from Middle English whit ‘white’ + eie ‘eye’ (Old English hwīt + ēage). Compare Blackie 2 and Greeney .
variant of   Witty  , with which this surname may have been confused.

variants.name: Whitson
meanings: English and Scottish (Midlothian): from an unrecorded Middle English personal name Whiteson, literally ‘white son’ or perhaps ‘son of someone named White ’. Occasionally, a variant of Whiston .

variants.name: Whitt
meanings: English (Nottinghamshire): variant of   White  .
Possibly also an Americanized form of German   Witt  .

variants.name: McQuitty
meanings: Irish (northern): Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Fhaoitigh, a patronymic from a Gaelicization of English White . See also McWhite .

variants.name: Whitecotton
meanings: Apparently a compound of   White   +   Cotton  , but the history is obscure.

variants.name: McElwain
meanings: Scottish and Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Giolla Bháin (Irish) or Mac Gille Bháin (Scottish) ‘son of the fair-haired lad’, often translated into English as White or Whyte but otherwise indistinguishable, once Anglicized, from Scottish Gaelic Mac Gille Bheathain in 2 below. Compare Kilbane .
Scottish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Gille Bheathain ‘son of the servant (i.e. devotee) of (Saint) Beathan’, a personal name representing a diminutive of beatha ‘life’. Compare McBain .

variants.name: McWhite
meanings: Irish: partly translated Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Faoitigh, a patronymic from a Gaelic form of English White .

variants.name: Wight
meanings: Scottish and English: nickname from Middle English wiht, wight ‘agile, nimble, strong, brave’ (Old Norse víg with the Old Norse neuter suffix -t, for which compare the word scant), or from the corresponding Older Scots word wicht. Possibly sometimes a nickname from Middle English wight ‘living creature; small portion or thing’, or from the corresponding Older Scots word wicht.

English: habitational name from the Isle of Wight. The island is recorded as Vectis (its Latin name) c. 150 AD . Its name derives from a British word, perhaps related to Welsh gwaith ‘turn, course’, meaning ‘place of the division’, in reference to the island's position in the Solent.
English and Scottish: variant of   White  .

variants.name: Whites
meanings: English: variant of White with genitive or post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Whitman
meanings: English (southern): from the Middle English personal name Whytman (Old English Hwītmann, from hwīt ‘white’ + mann ‘man’). Compare White 1 and also Wightman , with which this surname was probably confused.
History: John Whitman settled in Weymouth, MA, c. 1638. The poet Walt Whitman (1819–92) was descended from Joseph Whitman, who had settled in Stratford, CT, from England around 1660.

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

en.lifeSummary: When Robert White was born in 1501, in Christchurch, Hampshire, England, his father, Sir Henry White, was 23 and his mother, Elizabeth Fenrother, was 21. He married Catherine Barrett in 1520, in Christchurch, Hampshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 8 daughters. He died in 1588, in Christchurch, Hampshire, England, at the age of 87.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Robert White est né en 1501, à Christchurch, Hampshire, Angleterre, son père, Sir Henry White, avait 23 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Fenrother, avait 21 ans. Il a épousé Catherine Barrett en 1520, à Christchurch, Hampshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 8 filles. Il est décédé en 1588, à Christchurch, Hampshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 87 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Robert White 1501 in Christchurch, Hampshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir Henry White 23 und seine Mutter Elizabeth Fenrother 21. Er hat 1520 in Christchurch, Hampshire, England, United Kingdom Catherine Barrett geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 8 Töchter. Er starb 1588 im Alter von 87 Jahren in Christchurch, Hampshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Robert White è nato il nel 1501 nel Christchurch, Hampshire, England, il suo padre, Sir Henry White, aveva 23 anni e il suo madre, Elizabeth Fenrother, aveva 21 anni. Lui sposato Catherine Barrett nel 1520, nel Christchurch, Hampshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 8 figlie. Morì nel 1588, nel Christchurch, Hampshire, England, all'età di 87 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Robert Whiteが1501年にイギリスハンプシュアイアチリストチャーチで生まれた時，父Sir Henry Whiteは23歳，母Elizabeth Fenrotherは21歳であった。1520年にイギリスイギリスハンプシュアイアチリストチャーチでCatherine Barrettと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と8人の娘の親であった。彼は1588年にイギリスハンプシュアイアチリストチャーチで87歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Robert White(이)가 1501년 영국 함프실르 크리스트c후르치에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Henry White(은)는 23세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth Fenrother(은)는 21세였다. 그는 1520년 영국 잉글랜드 함프실르 크리스트c후르치에서 Catherine Barrett(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 여덟 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1588년 영국 함프실르 크리스트c후르치에서 87세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Robert White nasceu em 1501, em Christchurch, Hampshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Henry White, tinha 23 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth Fenrother, tinha 21 anos. Ele casou-se com Catherine Barrett em 1520, em Christchurch, Hampshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 8 filhas. Ele faleceu em 1588, em Christchurch, Hampshire, Inglaterra, com 87 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Robert White родился в 1501 г. в местности крисчурч, хампшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir Henry White было 23 года, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Fenrother, 21. Он женился на женщине по имени Catherine Barrett в 1520 г. в местности крисчурч, хампшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 8 дочерей. Он умер в 1588 г. в местности крисчурч, хампшир, Англия в возрасте 87 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Robert White nació en 1501, en Christchurch, Hampshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir Henry White, tenía 23 años y su mamá, Elizabeth Fenrother, tenía 21 años. Se casó con Catherine Barrett en 1520, en Christchurch, Hampshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 8 hijas. Murió en 1588, en Christchurch, Hampshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 87 años.
